city_intro.label: City Intro
city_intro.description: Standalone SEO intro (H2 + 2 paragraphs, 160-190 words) about the service in the target city.
city_intro.ai_prompt:
You are writing SEO content for {business}, a trained, locally operated pest control company serving {city}, {state}. Target keyword: "{service} {city}". Write a city intro section: 1. heading_text (H2): 6-10 words, includes "{city}" and references {service} or local demand for it. 2. text: exactly 2 flowing paragraphs, 160-190 words total. - Para 1: Why homeowner in {city} need {service}, grounded in the local climate and seasonal pest cycles. - Para 2: How {business} helps with {service} specifically — approach, thoroughness, and follow-up. Keep the focus on {service}; you may briefly note they also handle other pest problems, but do NOT list them. End forward-looking. Use "{city}" 2-3 times. No bullets, no headings inside text. Wrap each paragraph in

...

. ACCURACY RULES (critical): Use only well-established general knowledge about {city}'s region, climate, and seasons. Do NOT invent or state as fact: statistics, prices, pass/success rates, review counts, years in business, awards, certifications, specific ZIP codes, specific neighborhood names, local ordinances, or any local detail you are not confident is true. If you are unsure about a specific detail for {city}, write about the broader region or state instead of inventing specifics. Never guarantee outcomes. Use the standard two-letter abbreviation for {state} where an abbreviation reads naturally. Do not make pesticide safety or efficacy claims beyond general statements. Never guarantee total or permanent eradication. Do not name specific chemical products. Tone: reassuring and professional, with appropriate urgency for active infestations. Write in original wording; do not copy competitor phrasing. LOCAL AREAS - the real, verified {city} neighborhoods you ARE permitted to name (pick only a few, not all): {neighborhoods} These provided names are the ONE exception to the accuracy rules above about not naming neighborhoods. If that line lists actual names, you MUST choose ONLY 2 OR 3 of them - never 4 or more, even though more are listed - and name those BY NAME as natural in-sentence examples - always woven into prose, NEVER a bulleted or "we serve X, Y, Z" list, and never add any name not on that line. If that line is blank or still shows the literal token {neighborhoods}, name NO specific neighborhoods, streets, or ZIP codes and speak only in general terms about area types. Wrap paragraphs in

...

. Return JSON only: {"heading_text": "...", "text": "

...

...

"} Related search terms for this page: {secondary_keywords}. Where it reads naturally, work a few of these variant phrasings into the copy. Prioritize readability — never list keywords, repeat them, or stuff them; if a term does not fit naturally, leave it out.

hero_subtext.label: Hero Subtext (Inject)
hero_subtext.description: Injects 2-3 city-specific sentences into the next hero_split block's hs_subtext field.
hero_subtext.ai_prompt:
You are writing hero subtext for {business}, a trained, locally operated pest control company, for a page targeting {city}, {state}. Write 2-3 sentences (40-60 words total): - Open with a {city}-specific statement grounded in the local climate and seasonal pest cycles. - Center on {service} — the specific problem {business} solves on this page. Do NOT list other pest types. - Close with a value statement for homeowner. No bullets. No exclamation marks. Professional, not sales-y. ACCURACY RULES (critical): Use only well-established general knowledge about {city}'s region, climate, and seasons. Do NOT invent or state as fact: statistics, prices, pass/success rates, review counts, years in business, awards, certifications, specific ZIP codes, specific neighborhood names, local ordinances, or any local detail you are not confident is true. If you are unsure about a specific detail for {city}, write about the broader region or state instead of inventing specifics. Never guarantee outcomes. Use the standard two-letter abbreviation for {state} where an abbreviation reads naturally. Do not make pesticide safety or efficacy claims beyond general statements. Never guarantee total or permanent eradication. Do not name specific chemical products. Tone: reassuring and professional, with appropriate urgency for active infestations. Write in original wording; do not copy competitor phrasing. LOCAL AREAS - the real, verified {city} neighborhoods you ARE permitted to name (pick only a few, not all): {neighborhoods} These provided names are the ONE exception to the accuracy rules above about not naming neighborhoods. If that line lists actual names, you MUST choose ONLY 2 OR 3 of them - never 4 or more, even though more are listed - and name those BY NAME as natural in-sentence examples - always woven into prose, NEVER a bulleted or "we serve X, Y, Z" list, and never add any name not on that line. If that line is blank or still shows the literal token {neighborhoods}, name NO specific neighborhoods, streets, or ZIP codes and speak only in general terms about area types. Wrap paragraphs in

...

. Return JSON only: {"text": "2-3 sentences here"}

feature_columns_local.label: Feature Columns - Local (Standalone)
feature_columns_local.description: 4-column feature_columns block: 3 core benefits + 1 city-specific column.
feature_columns_local.ai_prompt:
You are writing feature columns for {business}, a trained, locally operated pest control company, for a {city}, {state} page. fc_heading: 6-10 words, city-aware and benefit-focused (e.g. "Why {city} homeowner Choose {business}"). Write exactly 4 columns: - Columns 1-3: core benefits of {business}'s {service} - things like experience, responsiveness, guarantees, thorough treatment, and trained technicians. Do NOT list other pest types. - Column 4: a {city}-specific benefit grounded in the local climate and seasonal pest cycles. Each column: heading 3-5 words (benefit-focused); text 20-30 words, concrete. ACCURACY RULES (critical): Use only well-established general knowledge about {city}'s region, climate, and seasons. Do NOT invent or state as fact: statistics, prices, pass/success rates, review counts, years in business, awards, certifications, specific ZIP codes, specific neighborhood names, local ordinances, or any local detail you are not confident is true. If you are unsure about a specific detail for {city}, write about the broader region or state instead of inventing specifics. Never guarantee outcomes. Use the standard two-letter abbreviation for {state} where an abbreviation reads naturally. Do not make pesticide safety or efficacy claims beyond general statements. Never guarantee total or permanent eradication. Do not name specific chemical products. Tone: reassuring and professional, with appropriate urgency for active infestations. Write in original wording; do not copy competitor phrasing. LOCAL AREAS - the real, verified {city} neighborhoods you ARE permitted to name (pick only a few, not all): {neighborhoods} These provided names are the ONE exception to the accuracy rules above about not naming neighborhoods. If that line lists actual names, you MUST choose ONLY 2 OR 3 of them - never 4 or more, even though more are listed - and name those BY NAME as natural in-sentence examples - always woven into prose, NEVER a bulleted or "we serve X, Y, Z" list, and never add any name not on that line. If that line is blank or still shows the literal token {neighborhoods}, name NO specific neighborhoods, streets, or ZIP codes and speak only in general terms about area types. Wrap paragraphs in

...

. Return JSON only: {"fc_heading": "...", "columns": [{"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}]} Related search terms for this page: {secondary_keywords}. Where it reads naturally, work a few of these variant phrasings into the copy. Prioritize readability — never list keywords, repeat them, or stuff them; if a term does not fit naturally, leave it out.

faq_local.label: FAQ - Local (Full Set)
faq_local.description: Generates a full set of 8 distinct service + local FAQ items, replacing the block's fq_items.
faq_local.ai_prompt:
You are writing FAQ content for {business}, a trained, locally operated pest control company, for a {city}, {state} page. Write 8 DISTINCT FAQ items a {city} homeowner searching for {service} would ask. Every question AND answer must be about {service} specifically — do NOT ask about other pest types or unrelated services. The 8 questions must each cover a DIFFERENT intent — no two may be rephrasings of the same question. Choose 8 distinct ones from these real buyer questions: what the {service} process involves and what to expect; how long it takes and how many visits are typical; how quickly {business} can respond; whether treatment is safe for children and pets; what affects the cost and whether free estimates are offered (NEVER state specific prices or dollar amounts); the local seasonal timing for {service} and when the problem peaks in this region; the signs a home needs {service}; professional {service} versus DIY products; how to prepare before a visit; how to prevent the problem from returning; whether the work is guaranteed and what happens if the problem comes back. Mix evergreen service questions with local/seasonal ones grounded in {city}'s climate and pest cycles. Roughly half the questions should naturally include "{city}"; the rest stay general to {service}. Each item: - question: a distinct, natural search-style question (8-15 words). - answer: 40-80 words, specific and genuinely helpful — no filler. ACCURACY RULES (critical): Use only well-established general knowledge about {city}'s region, climate, and seasons. Do NOT invent or state as fact: statistics, prices, pass/success rates, review counts, years in business, awards, certifications, specific ZIP codes, local ordinances, or any local detail you are not confident is true. If unsure about a specific detail for {city}, write about the broader region or state instead of inventing specifics. Never guarantee outcomes or total/permanent eradication. Use the standard two-letter abbreviation for {state} where it reads naturally. Do not make pesticide safety or efficacy claims beyond general statements, and do not name specific chemical products. Tone: reassuring and professional, with appropriate urgency for active infestations. Write in original wording; do not copy competitor phrasing. LOCAL AREAS - the real, verified {city} neighborhoods you ARE permitted to name: {neighborhoods} These are the ONE exception to the accuracy rules about naming neighborhoods. Across the ENTIRE 8-item set, reference a specific neighborhood in AT MOST 2 answers, and use only 2-3 names total drawn from that line — always woven naturally into prose, NEVER a bulleted or "we serve X, Y, Z" list, and never add a name not on that line. If that line is blank or still shows the literal token {neighborhoods}, name NO specific neighborhoods, streets, or ZIP codes and speak only in general area terms. Wrap each answer's paragraph(s) in

...

. Return JSON only: {"items": [{"question": "...", "answer": "..."}, ... exactly 8 objects ...]} Related search terms for this page: {secondary_keywords}. Make sure the FAQ set collectively covers the distinct intents behind these variant phrasings (each is usually a different question a searcher would ask). Phrase questions the way a real homeowner would — never keyword-stuff, and skip any term that does not map to a natural question.

seasonal_calendar.label: Seasonal Calendar (Standalone)
seasonal_calendar.description: 4-step seasonal breakdown of pressure/demand by season for the region. Opt-in for seasonal niches (pest, wildlife, remediation).
seasonal_calendar.ai_prompt:
You are writing a seasonal guide for {business}, a trained, locally operated pest control company, for {city}, {state}. steps_heading: 5-9 words referencing seasons in {city} and {service}. Write exactly 4 items, one per season (Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter): - heading: the season plus a 2-4 word theme. - text: 25-40 words on what homeowner in {city}'s region typically face that season, grounded in the local climate and seasonal pest cycles, and how {business} helps. Base seasonal patterns on the region's real climate. Do not invent specific data. ACCURACY RULES (critical): Use only well-established general knowledge about {city}'s region, climate, and seasons. Never guarantee total or permanent eradication. Do not name specific chemical products. Tone: reassuring and professional, with appropriate urgency for active infestations. Write in original wording; do not copy competitor phrasing. LOCAL AREAS - the real, verified {city} neighborhoods you ARE permitted to name (pick only a few, not all): {neighborhoods} These provided names are the ONE exception to the accuracy rules above about not naming neighborhoods. If that line lists actual names, you MUST choose ONLY 2 OR 3 of them - never 4 or more, even though more are listed - and name those BY NAME as natural in-sentence examples - always woven into prose, NEVER a bulleted or "we serve X, Y, Z" list, and never add any name not on that line. If that line is blank or still shows the literal token {neighborhoods}, name NO specific neighborhoods, streets, or ZIP codes and speak only in general terms about area types. Wrap paragraphs in

. Return JSON only: {"steps_heading": "...", "steps_items": [{"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}]}
